claim: BLEURT uses contextual embeddings to capture semantic similarity beyond surface matching.
evidence: BERTScore computes a similarity score for each token in the candidate sentence with each token in the reference sentence. Instead of exact matches, we compute token similarity using contextual embeddings.

claim: BERTScore is a learned metric robust to distribution shifts.
evidence: Our insight is that it is possible to combine expressivity and robustness by pre-training a fully learned metric on large amounts of synthetic data before fine-tuning it on human ratings. To this end, we introduce BLEURT, a text generation metric based on BERT.

claim: Kim and Lipani only predict satisfaction scores and do not generate user utterances.
evidence: We propose a deep learning-based user simulator that predicts users satisfaction scores and actions while also jointly generating users utterances in a multi-task manner for goal-oriented dialogue systems.

claim: Lin et al. introduced GenTUS as a rule-based user simulator.
evidence: User simulators are commonly used to train task-oriented dialogue systems via reinforcement learning. In this work, we propose a generative transformer-based user simulator (GenTUS).

claim: LoRA updates all pretrained model weights during fine-tuning.
evidence: LoRA freezes the pre-trained model weights and injects trainable rank decomposition matrices into each layer of the Transformer architecture, greatly reducing the number of trainable parameters for downstream tasks.

claim: It is possible to finetune a 7B parameter model on a single 48GB GPU.
evidence: We demonstrate for the first time that it is possible to finetune a 65B parameter model on a single 48GB GPU while preserving full 16-bit finetuning task performance.

claim: WildChat is a corpus of synthetic dialogues generated by GPT-4.
evidence: We introduce WildChat, a corpus of 1M real user-ChatGPT interaction logs collected in the wild.

claim: Schema-Guided Dialogue contains over 100k task-oriented conversations.
evidence: The Schema-Guided Dialogue dataset consists of over 16k multi-domain task-oriented conversations spanning 16 domains.

claim: DialoGPT was trained on 147M conversation-like exchanges from Reddit comment chains spanning 2010 to 2017.
evidence: Trained on 147M conversation-like exchanges extracted from Reddit comment chains over a period spanning from 2005 through 2017, DialoGPT extends the Hugging Face PyTorch transformer implementation.

claim: SimpleTOD uses a masked language model for task-oriented dialogue subtasks.
evidence: SimpleTOD is a simple approach to task-oriented dialogue that uses a single, causal language model trained on all sub-tasks recast as a single sequence prediction problem.

claim: SimpleTOD improves inform rate by 8.1 points and success rate by 19.7 points in an end-to-end setting.
evidence: SimpleTOD improves the main metrics used to evaluate action decisions and response generation in an end-to-end setting: inform rate by 8.1 points, success rate by 9.7 points, and combined score.

claim: The big Transformer achieved 38.4 BLEU on WMT 2014 English-to-German translation.
evidence: On the WMT 2014 English-to-German translation task, the big transformer model outperforms the best previously reported models by more than 2.0 BLEU, establishing a new state-of-the-art BLEU score of 28.4.

claim: Models quantized by VPTQ result in 3 times improvement in inference throughput compared to SOTA.
evidence: The models quantized by VPTQ result in 1.6 to 1.8 times improvement in inference throughput compared to SOTA.

claim: Qwen2.5 open-weight models include a 405B parameter model.
evidence: The open-weight offerings include base models and instruction-tuned models in sizes of 0.5B, 1.5B, 3B, 7B, 14B, 32B, and 72B parameters.

claim: Qwen2.5 increased pre-training data to 8 trillion tokens.
evidence: The pre-training data increased from 7 trillion tokens to 18 trillion tokens, with focus on knowledge, coding, math, multilinguality, and long-context data.

claim: Qwen2.5-Turbo supports context length up to 2 million tokens.
evidence: In addition, Qwen2.5-Turbo supports a context length of up to 1 million tokens.

claim: The NeurIPS 2019 Reproducibility Challenge had 74 papers claimed for reproduction.
evidence: The NeurIPS 2019 Reproducibility Challenge used OpenReview to enable communication between authors and challenge participants. A total of 173 papers were claimed for reproduction.
